articles.title: JS Crossword: All the Answers are JavaScript
articles.description: This hand-crafted puzzle will seriously stretch your JavaScript knowledge. I’ve seen so many people on social media either cheering having finished it or cursing being stuck…
articles.author: Lyra Rebane

articles.title: Staged Publishing for npm Packages Goes Live
articles.description: npm’s ‘staged publishing’ model provides a review period with approval required before packages go live on the npm registry. Both npm 11.15.0 and pnpm 11.3 have added support.
articles.author: The npm Project

articles.title: Chrome Previews Declarative Partial Updates
articles.description: Two new sets of APIs for working with HTML out-of-order, whether in the doc itself (via <template for>) or through dynamic insertion (via setHTML/streamHTML). Experimental in Chrome 148 but polyfills exist.
articles.author: Pollard and Rosenthal (Chrome)

articles.title: Optimizing Our Build Times by Migrating from Webpack to Rspack
articles.description: How Yelp cut build times in half with Rspack (a Rust-powered drop-in replacement for webpack) and what they learned about barrel files and re-exports.
articles.author: Benson Pan (Yelp)

articles.title: Microsoft's Analysis of the Recent Mini Shai-Hulud Compromise
articles.description: A detailed post-mortem of last week’s ‘Mini Shai-Hulud’ npm security chain compromise.
articles.author: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

articles.title: Deno 2.8: The 'Biggest Minor Release' to Date
articles.description: The headline is that Node.js compatibility has jumped from 42% in Deno 2.7 to 76.4% now (higher than Bun). Deno also gets huge perf gains across the board and drops the npm: prefix requirement when adding/installing packages. Plus many other things, including:
articles.author:
New commands including: audit fix to upgrade vulnerable dependencies, pack to build a project into a npm-publishable tarball, and why to see why a package has been installed.

Web API support improvements, including OffscreenCanvas going stable.
Node module.registerHooks() support.
Chrome DevTools can now inspect Deno's network traffic.
A built-in CPU profiler.

import defer support.
V8 14.9 and TypeScript 6.0.3.

articles.title: 📄 DOCX 9.7: Build Word .docx Files in JavaScript
articles.description: A mature, comprehensive library for generating docx files both client and server side. There’s a CodePen-based example with the basics, plus over 100 example scripts.
articles.author: Dolan Miu

articles.title: tinykeys 4.0: A Tiny, Modern Library for Keybindings
articles.description: Has a very simple, straightforward API and the page includes live demos.
articles.author: Jamie Kyle

articles.title: An Official Node.js Codemod to Migrate from Axios to fetch
articles.description: A codemod to transform code using Axios to using the Fetch API instead.
articles.author: The Node.js Team

articles.title: 📱 Hot Updater: Self-Hosted Over-the-Air Updates for React Native
articles.description: A way to deploy updates to apps without app store submissions.
articles.author: Sungyu Kang

articles.description: Firefox has added Web Serial support in Firefox 151 so you can connect to microcontrollers, 3D printers, and other serial-connected hardware from JS.
Mozilla's Ryan Hunt says goodbye to asm.js as Firefox's JS engine now disables optimizations for it by default.
🤖 Modern Web Guidance is a suite of expert-vetted skills to guide agents in building modern web experiences, e.g. "begin preloading pages when users hover over important links."
TC39 held its latest meeting last week – a roundup is due soon.
Storybook 10.4 – the frontend component workshop – has added first-class TanStack React support and the ability for agents to set up Storybook in complex apps.
Node.js v26.2.0 (Current) and v24.16.0 (LTS)
pnpm 11.3 – Adds pnpm stage for staged publishing, trustLockfile for setting controls around application of trust policies, native pkg, repo and set-script commands & more.
npm 12.0 Prerelease, Apache ECharts 6.1 (Demos.)

articles.title: The State of CSS Centering in 2026
articles.description: Temani offers up a fresh exploration of a perennially popular CSS topic, promising some hidden tricks and modern features you may not be aware of, especially now that things like place-content and CSS anchor positioning are in play.
articles.author: Temani Afif

articles.title: 🗳️ The State of CSS Survey for 2026 is Now Open
articles.description: This year’s edition covers fewer features, meaning you should be able to get through it quicker. It focuses “on the ones that matter most”, adding that in an age of AI-assisted coding “maybe doing less — but doing it better — is how those of us who still insist on hand-coding [..] can stay competitive”.
articles.author: Devographics

articles.title: What's Missing in CSS Layout
articles.description: Patrick, drawing from the results of last year’s State of CSS survey, explores the friction points of modern web design and layout, looking at what gaps are left for CSS to fill.
articles.author: Patrick Brosset

articles.title: ▶  Google Might Have Just Killed Websites
articles.description: Post I/O, Kevin talks about the inflection point we find ourselves at, with AI scraping devaluing websites en masse, and AI summaries removing the need to click — asking what this means long term. There’s related reading to be found in the YouTube description.
articles.author: Kevin Powell

articles.title: CSS vs. JavaScript
articles.description: When it comes to animation performance, which performs better, and when? Josh takes a look at the nuances involved and the performance implications of different animation strategies.
articles.author: Josh W. Comeau

articles.title: Chrome Introduces Declarative Partial Updates
articles.description: Chrome 148 introduces (behind a flag) two experimental features for declarative partial updates: <template for> brings JS-free templating into your markup, while setHTML() and streamHTML() lets you safely stream HTML blocks into the DOM out of document order. Polyfills are offered for both.
articles.author: Pollard and Rosenthal (Chrome)

articles.title: Maybe Don’t Rely on Google’s “Modern Web Guidance”
articles.description: Last week we shared news of Google’s new set of LLM skills designed to help build modern web apps. Adrian has some thoughts, recommending we “probably ignore” for… a few reasons.
articles.author: Adrian Roselli

articles.title: Don't Put aria-label on Generic Elements Like divs
articles.description: Explains why slapping aria-label on a plain <div> or <span> is both a spec violation and tricky in practice. There are exceptions, but screen readers across browsers and operating systems show wildly inconsistent results.
articles.author: Manuel Matuzovic

articles.title: Framework-Agnostic Design Systems: A Practical Approach to Web Components
articles.description: An accessible guide that covers managing, building and packaging design system components.
articles.author: Scott Riley

articles.title: Tegaki: Turn Any Font Into Animated Handwriting
articles.description: Supports any font or text (including non-English) and works with all major frameworks. A bunch of handwriting fonts are built into the package and you can try it live in this playground. Neat!
articles.author: Gokhan Kurt

articles.title: font-size-adjust-calculator: A Font Metrics Calculator for the font-size-adjust Property
articles.description: A straightforward, no-frills tool — just upload a font in TTF, OTF, WOFF, or WOFF2 format, and plug in some optional variable font settings, and it will provide a suitable value for the font-size-adjust property.
articles.author: richard rutter

articles.title: Tailwind CSSTree: CSSTree Syntax for Tailwind
articles.description: A JS package that allows you to use the syntax from CSSTree (a parser/walker/generator for CSS) in Tailwind projects. You can use it directly with CSSTree and it's compatible with the ESLint CSS Plugin.
articles.author: Nicholas C. Zakas

articles.title: dev.css: CSS to Make Any Plain HTML Modern and Responsive
articles.description: Inspired by new.css, a set of default styles for standard HTML – potentially useful for a simple blog, an “about” site, or similar. Demo page.
articles.author: Devin

articles.title: JSON For You: JSON Visualization and Processing Tool
articles.description: Includes graph and table view modes, nested parsing, support for jq, and ability to import/export CSV. We see a lot of tools like this but the output here (above) is particularly elegant. Try it here.
articles.author: loggerhead
